content.rendered:
Got a long weekend on the horizon or looking to shake off the routine with a spontaneous city break? Europe is full of beautiful cities just a short flight away, each offering its own unique charm and attractions. Here’s our pick of the top five last-minute city breaks in Europe – all you need to do is pack a bag, check out these destinations, and prepare for an unforgettable getaway!

1. Lisbon, Portugal

Portugal’s capital is a fantastic option for a quick getaway, offering sunshine, stunning architecture, and vibrant culture. The picturesque tram rides through the historic Alfama district, the iconic Belém Tower, and the lively Bairro Alto district make Lisbon feel like a true escape.

  • Seasonal Attractions: Visit in autumn or spring to enjoy milder weather and fewer crowds. The annual Lisbon Jazz Festival in November is perfect for music lovers.
  • Money-Saving Tips: Get a Lisboa Card for unlimited public transport and free or discounted entry to top attractions.

Get your euros from Travel FX for the best rate before you go, so you’re all set for those café breaks and tram rides.

2. Rome, Italy

Rome, the Eternal City, is always a good idea. From iconic landmarks like the Colosseum and the Vatican to quaint neighbourhoods like Trastevere, Rome is a feast for the senses. Don’t miss the gelato shops on every corner and try your hand at some Italian phrases!

  • Tourist Attractions: Don’t miss a wander around the Vatican Museums, but book tickets online to avoid long queues. Visit in winter or autumn to enjoy fewer crowds.
  • Money-Saving Tips: Opt for the Roma Pass to get free entry to two top attractions and unlimited travel on the city’s public transport.

Get your euros from Travel FX for a stress-free currency solution before you fly, leaving you free to enjoy every Roman espresso and slice of pizza.

3. Prague, Czech Republic

Prague is a gem that offers both history and vibrant nightlife. Stroll across the Charles Bridge, explore the medieval Old Town, and visit the magnificent Prague Castle. For those visiting in December, Prague’s Christmas Markets offer a magical experience.

  • Seasonal Attractions: Enjoy the city’s Gothic charm year-round, but don’t miss the Christmas Markets for mulled wine, gingerbread, and festive cheer.
  • Money-Saving Tips: Walk as much as possible! Prague’s main attractions are best explored on foot, and it’s the ideal way to soak up the city’s vibe.

Get your Czech koruna from Travel FX in advance to skip high exchange rates at airport kiosks.

4. Budapest, Hungary

For a mix of historical sites and hip culture, Budapest is the perfect spot. With thermal baths, scenic river cruises, and beautiful architecture, it’s ideal for a rejuvenating escape. The view of the Parliament Building along the Danube at sunset is unforgettable.

  • Tourist Attractions: Spend an afternoon relaxing in the Széchenyi Baths and stroll down Andrássy Avenue, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
  • Money-Saving Tips: Choose a Budapest Card for free transport, discounts, and entry to museums.

Get your Hungarian forints from Travel FX and avoid unnecessary bank fees while exploring one of Europe’s most affordable capitals.

5. Amsterdam, Netherlands

Amsterdam’s canals, world-class museums, and quirky cafes make it a go-to spot for a relaxed city break. Rent a bike to explore the city like a local, visit the Van Gogh Museum, and take a peaceful canal cruise.

  • Seasonal Attractions: Spring is the perfect time to visit Amsterdam if you want to see the famous tulip fields. Alternatively, November is quieter but still full of charm.
  • Money-Saving Tips: A Museumkaart gives access to many of Amsterdam’s top museums, including the Van Gogh and Rijksmuseum, for a fraction of the price.

Get your euros from Travel FX to avoid the higher exchange rates at airport kiosks and enjoy Amsterdam’s cafes, museums, and shops.

Final Tip: Always book accommodation directly through the hotel’s website or reputable booking platforms to score the best rates. And don’t forget to take local currency along for hassle-free spending – pre-order from Travel FX to get the best rates on your euros, koruna, or forints before you head off.

Happy travels!

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
